configurations.name: generate from instructions
args: Generate multiple-choice questions specifically about Python regular expressions using the 're' module. Focus on questions requiring either synthesis (writing a regex pattern based on a description) or analysis (determining what a given regex pattern matches or does). Cover common concepts like character classes, quantifiers, grouping, anchors, lookarounds, and standard 're' functions (e.g., search, match, findall, sub).

Ensure questions accurately test Python regex synthesis or analysis as requested. Verify the correctness of regex patterns, expected matches, and explanations. Ensure distractors are plausible but incorrect applications or interpretations of regex concepts.

This is not a true course lecture, just some introductory material about the course and the environment, so the questions will just be an example for the students to see what kind of questions they will be answering in the course.

Focus on the interpretation and synthesis of regular expressions, or python code for working with text, the beautifulsoup library, and the spacy library. Make the questions hard (e.g. difficult regex) but concise, but do not make them hard in a way where they have to know the complete API of the libraries.
Tend towards shorter questions and answers.
